commands.review_summary:
The PR rewrites the bundled 1Password skill and get-started reference to run `op` directly for service-account and desktop-app auth, keep tmux only for standalone signin, and correct per-platform desktop IPC wording.

PR surface: Docs +46. Total +46 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes for source-level behavior: current main still instructs tmux-only 1Password usage, and the linked report plus PR proof show the desktop-app path works with direct `op` calls; I did not run a live 1Password desktop setup myself.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This PR is useful and proof-backed, but the remaining action is branch conflict resolution plus maintainer review rather than a separate ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: fix(skills/1password): stop forcing tmux for desktop app auth (#52540)
target.body:
## Summary

The bundled `1password` skill instructed agents to wrap every `op` invocation in a tmux session. That blanket requirement breaks the **desktop app** integration path: the 1Password CLI desktop-app integration uses a per-user IPC channel (XPC on macOS via the Browser Helper, a Unix domain socket on Linux, a named pipe on Windows) established for the gateway's exec environment, and that channel is not reliably reachable from a freshly-spawned tmux subshell. Agents that follow the skill in desktop-app mode see early steps succeed but later `op` calls fail.

This PR splits the skill guidance by auth mode:

- **Service account** (`OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN` set): run `op` directly, no tmux.
- **Desktop app** (no service account, app integration enabled): run `op` directly so it inherits the host IPC channel.
- **Standalone signin** (CLI-only interactive `op signin --account ...`): keep tmux only here, and fix the example to `eval "$(op signin ...)"`. The previous example printed the eval-style export but never applied it, so subsequent `op whoami`/`op vault list` would fail with no exported `OP_SESSION_*`.

Also corrects the per-platform IPC description in the skill body and `references/get-started.md`: previous text described a single "per-user Unix domain socket" globally, which is misleading on macOS (XPC) and Windows (named pipe).

Rebased onto current `main` (was ~12k commits behind); resolved the `SKILL.md` header conflict and dropped the contributor `CHANGELOG.md` entry per repo policy.

## Real behavior proof

I now have a host with paired 1Password **desktop-app** integration, so the desktop-app claim is verified live. All account-identifying data below is redacted.

```
Behavior addressed: Skill text forced `tmux` around every `op` call, which breaks the desktop-app IPC path; the standalone-signin example also failed to `eval` the session export.
Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 25.5.0), 1Password desktop app with CLI integration enabled, op 2.33.1. Shell was NOT inside tmux ($TMUX unset) and had NO OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN set (i.e. exercising the desktop-app path exactly as the rewritten skill describes).
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  $ echo "TMUX=${TMUX:-<unset>}  OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN=${OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN:+set}"
  TMUX=<unset>  OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN=<unset>
  $ op --version
  2.33.1
  $ op vault list      # direct exec, no tmux
  $ op whoami          # direct exec, no tmux
Evidence after fix (redacted):
  $ op vault list
  ID                            NAME
  <redacted-id-1>               <redacted-vault-1>
  <redacted-id-2>               <redacted-vault-2>
  ... (8 vaults returned)
  $ op whoami
  URL:        https://<redacted-account>.1password.com/
  Email:      <redacted>@<redacted>
  User ID:    <redacted-user-id>
Observed result after fix: Both `op vault list` and `op whoami` succeed via direct exec with no tmux session and no service-account token, confirming the rewritten desktop-app section. (Idle-lock nuance: after the desktop app auto-locks, the first `op` call re-triggers system auth; once unlocked, subsequent calls work directly without tmux — tmux is not involved on this path.)
What was not tested live by the contributor: the Linux (Unix-domain-socket) and Windows (named-pipe) desktop-app transports — the per-platform mechanism differs but the agent-facing rule (run `op` directly, not via tmux) is identical; and a fresh standalone `op signin` flow, though the `eval` fix is mechanically the documented 1Password behavior.
```

## What else was verified

- Read the existing skill against 1Password CLI documented behavior (desktop-app integration IPC mechanism per platform, `op signin` eval-form output).
- Reviewed the diff against `skills/1password/SKILL.md` and `references/get-started.md` for consistency.
- No code, no schema, no test files changed; nothing to unit-test.

## Files changed

- `skills/1password/SKILL.md`
- `skills/1password/references/get-started.md`

No `CHANGELOG.md` entry, per repo policy (release-owned). Release-note context: *Skills/1password — stop forcing every `op` call through tmux; detect auth mode (service account / desktop app / standalone signin) and use tmux only for the standalone interactive signin path. Fixes #52540.*

## Risks

Low. Skill-only. Reverts the over-broad tmux requirement on two of three auth-mode paths; the third (standalone signin) keeps tmux and fixes a real `eval` bug. Agents that explicitly want tmux for their own session-recording can still wrap their own commands.

Linked issue: #52540
target.author: koshaji
